text: The surgeon begins the procedure on the right side by dissecting the adrenal tumor from the undersurface of the liver up to the diaphragm. The liver is separated, and attention is turned to the medial aspect of the tumor, creating a plane between the inferior vena cava and the tumor. Robotic instruments are used for precise dissection. The adrenal vein is identified, dissected, clipped with hemolog clips, and divided.

The surgeon dissects the undersurface of the adrenal tumor and the upper pole of the kidney, carefully clipping and dividing major vascular attachments to prevent bleeding. Bipolar energy and clips are used to ensure the vascular supply to the right kidney remains intact, while avoiding breaching the tumor capsule.

The surgeon carefully preserves normal adrenal tissue while managing the bilateral adrenal tumors. Hemlock clips and bipolar diathermy are used to control the vascular attachments of the pheochromocytomas, which are inherently very vascular. The presence of a second main adrenal vein draining into the inferior vena cava postrolaterally is noted.

The surgeon has preserved the normal adrenal tissue to the maximum extent possible. The specimen is packed and placed under the left lobe of the liver for easy retrieval during the opposite side operation. Adequate hemostasis is achieved using Surgicel febrile to manage low pressure, low volume bleeding.

The robotic instruments are repositioned for surgery on the left side. The surgeon dissects the adrenal gland by separating surrounding structures, including the spleen, tail of the pancreas, upper pole of the kidney, and colon, without directly handling the adrenal. The adrenal vein is looped and controlled to spare it during the adrenal-sparing surgery.

The surgeon dissects the tumor-bearing lateral limb of the left adrenal gland, performing enucleoresection of the tumor using a combination of blunt and sharp dissection techniques. Hemolog clips are applied as needed to manage bleeding encountered during the procedure.
A significant portion of the left adrenal gland is preserved. Hemostasis is achieved, and a drain is placed. The specimen is packed and retrieved along with the right-sided adrenal gland.
